reviews.author: Ray Mak
reviews.text: Beautified Version: Po Nagar Temple is a truly beautiful ancient temple nestled in Nha Trang. I may not know all the details of its history, but the moment I stepped in, it reminded me of Angkor Wat in Cambodia — a place rich in soul, history, and quiet grandeur. The temple is remarkably well preserved, and its atmosphere feels both sacred and grounding. The entrance fee is 30,000 VND, which I find very reasonable. It goes toward maintaining the site and supporting the staff who take care of this historical treasure. While there isn’t much “activity” in the modern sense, the beauty of this place lies in slowing down — learning about its history, admiring ancient artifacts, and taking in the architecture. Many visitors also come here to make wishes or offer their own prayers. Near the exit, you’ll find a few local stalls selling souvenirs and light snacks, adding a nice local touch to the experience. If you enjoy history, ancient architecture, or simply appreciating places with deep cultural roots, Po Nagar Temple is well worth a visit. If you’re lucky, you may even catch one of the traditional dance performances held here from time to time — a beautiful way to see the culture come alive.

reviews.author: michael
reviews.text: This is a great temple. Fascinating look into Cham culture and history. The ancient towers are beautifully preserved. Wonderful gardens. Spent about three hours wondering around viewing the temples and grounds. Po Nagar provinces robes if you do not have proper clothing. It is a lot of fun to walk around on the many paths. There are a lot of areas to sit and meditate. Next time I will bring a lunch to I joy on the grounds. The inside of the temples are amazing. Good bathrooms, snacks bars and lots of places to sit. Very easy to find walking over from Nha Trang center.

reviews.author: G Whizz
reviews.text: Po Nagar Temple is a pleasant and worthwhile stop in Nha Trang. The Cham towers are well preserved and visually striking, offering a good glimpse into the area’s ancient history. The surrounding gardens are nicely kept and create a calm, enjoyable atmosphere, making it easy to spend some relaxed time walking around and taking photos. It’s not a very large site, but the combination of architecture, setting, and cultural significance makes it well worth a visit when exploring the city.

reviews.author: Giang Hoàng
reviews.text: Tháp Bà Pô Nagar is an ancient Cham temple complex located about 2–3 km north of Nha Trang, along the Sông Cái. If you have time, you can walk to the towers and enjoy the cool sea breeze along the way. The distance is not too far — you only need to cross one bridge. On the way, you can also stop by the Alexandre Yersin Monument, which is located along Trần Phú Street by the beach. During the tourist season, it’s recommended to travel by motorbike or walk, as it’s usually much faster than going by car. Since cars often get stuck in traffic on the way to the towers, especially since cars are restricted to a single lane. The entrance fee is 30,000 VND per person. Hope you have a wonderful experience when visiting this cultural and historical site.

reviews.author: Ric Dunn
reviews.text: This is a beautiful look into the past, but with little to no details provided about it on site it requires either a really good internet connection or a tour guide. There was a QE code to a flashy 360 style website with great views but the internet wasn’t strong enough for it to work properly. A great idea on paper, let down by details. The local performance at the venue was a welcome addition to the atmosphere Worth seeing to say you’ve seen something cultural but not overly impressive.

reviews.author: Philippe Tremblay
reviews.text: Really a great temple to see in the heart of Nha Trang. I recommend going at 6am when it open because it's really peaceful and beautiful with the sunrise. To enter the temples you need to have shoulder and knee cover. The entry price is 30 000 Dong. At a leassure pace it took us 1 hour to visit all the site. It is definitely worth it!

reviews.author: Monica Flux
reviews.text: We visited Po Nagar Temple during our trip to Nha Trang, and it was a fascinating glimpse into Cham culture and history. The ancient towers are beautifully preserved, with intricate carvings and a serene atmosphere that makes you feel transported back in time. The views from the hilltop are stunning, overlooking the Cai River, and the short hike up adds a nice touch of adventure. Learning about the Hindu influences and the goddess Ponagar was enlightening—definitely worth the entrance fee. That said, it can get quite crowded with tour groups, especially in the mornings, which made it a bit noisy and hard to fully appreciate the tranquility. Some areas could use better signage in English for non-Vietnamese speakers, and the steps might be challenging for those with mobility issues. I'd recommend it to anyone interested in Vietnam's diverse heritage, but try to go early or late to avoid the crowds!

reviews.author: Baktybala Torgautova
reviews.text: Very beautiful temple. Buddhist temple with Hindu elements. I liked that they didn’t painted and didn’t made fancy renovation. Looks very authentic and raw. A lot of space to set down, drink cool drinks, buy souvenirs. Recommend to walk around, had a small garden behind with beautiful trees and big rocks. There were dancers at the beginning who dance national dances with drums. Entrance fee 30.000 d. has a clean toilet near the temple. Beautiful view on the city. It was a bit busy with tourist groups, I was there around 10.40. Google maps says they open at 6, maybe it’s better to go as early as possible.

reviews.author: Barsbold Baatarsuren
reviews.text: A wonderful historic site and absolutely worth visiting, especially when the sky is clear. The ancient brick towers are stunning and offer great photo opportunities, with beautiful views over the river and city. The atmosphere is very calm and peaceful, making it a perfect place to slow down, reflect, and appreciate Vietnam’s deep history. It’s easy to walk around, not too crowded, and suitable for families as well. A must-see landmark in Nha Trang for anyone interested in culture, history, and photography.

reviews.author: Stephen Merrett
reviews.text: A charming and well maintained historical site. The buildings are well maintained and the surrounding gardens very pleasant with lots of quite little places to sit and relax. The 30,000 VND entrance is well worthwhile.

reviews.author: jimbo scannell
reviews.text: Walk here today. Arrived just when all the tour groups came(enuff said). Spent half a day absorbing the whole sprintual aspect of the religious site and felt an inner calm considering I'm a non faith believer.(Sounds like a line out of a song) Worth the 30k vdn entrance for the visit to enlighten ones soul and for some great photos you can take with your selfie stick or tripod.( Best you take snaps on the stairs as people climb them it's more rewarding). Enjoy I did.

reviews.author: WheelWalker Lucky
reviews.text: 1000 YEARS AGO VS. YESTERDAY: WHY PONAGAR IS STILL HYPED? ⛩️🔥 I’ve seen hundreds of temples, but Ponagar is different. Imagine bricks over a thousand years old held together without a single gram of cement. The secret is lost, yet the towers stand. This place isn't about religion; it's about raw energy. Traveler’s Tip: Arrive at 7:30 AM. By 9:00, it's a "battle for the shot" with tour groups. Secret Layer: The bricks change color based on humidity. Blood-red in the rain, fiery orange in the sun. #PoNagar #NhaTrang2026 #AncientSecrets #TravelExpert #VietnamHeritage

reviews.author: Denise Hernández- Wright
reviews.text: Very interesting and peaceful landmark, you have to pay an entry , they have long covers if you happen to be in shorts to enter the temples. In 30 mins you are done. We went end of day and it was not busy.

reviews.author: Hunter Reed
reviews.text: Very beautiful and peaceful! The grounds are well kept and make for great photos. If you forget long pants / respectful clothing, there are robes to wear for free

reviews.author: F Sufar
reviews.text: The temple is a beautiful building, surrounded by lush trees and blooming flowers. We loved the monuments and the spacious grounds. There's also a great selection of affordable souvenirs available. The entry ticket is very reasonable at 30,000 per person.

reviews.author: Akang
reviews.text: A must stop to appreciate the uniquely built temple, traditional dancing is performed there perically, definitely look for that. Very impressed.

reviews.author: Sally Ouyang
reviews.text: You must buy a ticket but it’s simple at the admission counter. Ticket is 30000 VND I believe. The temple will give you a free robe to wear to enter if you aren’t dressed appropriately! Make sure to take your shoes off when entering the temple!

reviews.author: Jolanta JAKUBOWSKA
reviews.text: Beautiful historical place. Located on the little hill. Surrounded by greenery with benches where you can relax. Beautiful view from the terace on the Nha Trang and sea. Definitely worth visiting. You can buy ticket on arrival 30000 v d.

reviews.author: Daria Miloslavskaya
reviews.text: I'm very grateful for visiting this amazing place... I felt like at home inside of the Temple... the presence of the Mother Goddess energy is strong... the history of this place is wonderful to know... I was with my guide and she told me a lot of interesting things about the place, a lot of gratitude too 🙏🏻 the tiket 🎟 is only 30 dongs

reviews.author: Kris Egerton
reviews.text: Nice place, reminds me of My Son ( nearby Hoi An), just in a better condition. And newer. Tickets can buy at the entrance. Every temple has a QR code and you can just listen the audio for free.

reviews.author: Lorane
reviews.text: Beautiful historical temple Po Nagar is aust visit while Nha Trang. Not to far out of the city and very photographic with a great story about the Cham people.

reviews.author: DUCKKI LEE
reviews.text: You can see everything in about 30 minutes to an hour, and still have time to spare. We recommend visiting early in the morning or late in the afternoon. The entrance fee is 30,000 dong.

reviews.author: Emil Sjöholm
reviews.text: Nice spot with a good view of the city. A small Entrance fee but it is worth it. Long clothes to borrow if you want to enter the temple. Lots of tourists.

reviews.author: Artur Pytel
reviews.text: One of the best looking and interesting places to visit in Nha Trang and I didn't see there so many Russians 😉🤣 Ticket costs 30k VND, you can visit wearing shorts, they will give you a long coat if you want to go inside the temple.

reviews.author: Kittikun C.
reviews.text: One of few non-beach places to visit in Nha Trang. I enjoy the scenery and overall vibe more than the temple itself.

reviews.author: Cao Vu Le
reviews.text: Po Nagar Cham Towers is a true testament to the Cham civilization. Not only rich in cultural and spiritual values, the heritage complex is also an excellent example of architectural beauty, which is enough to capture visitors’ hearts. Whether you’re a history buff, nature lover, or photography enthusiast, the Po Nagar site offers a bit of everything, making it the ultimate destination in Nha Trang.

reviews.author: Hamy Nguyen
reviews.text: This temple deserves to be visited if you happen to be in Nha Trang! It is so culturally rich and the architecture hints at the hinduistic influence of the Cham people. The temple is also very well restored and you can see which parts of the temple are new and which ones are the original. I would recommend visiting during the opening hours of the site (around 6:30-8AM) since it can also get quite crowded even in the weekdays. You can also get a guided tour which is short but informative so you can learn more about the history of the temple!

reviews.author: Diana Kim
reviews.text: The entrance fee is 30 000 dong. There was no queue for tickets. Atmosphere inside is stunning. Must-visit place in Nha trang!

reviews.author: Mariusz Redwanz
reviews.text: This is one of the few attractions in the city, and it’s worth a quick visit—especially since it’s close to the center and on the way to the hot springs. The ticket is only 30,000 VND. Technically, you’re not supposed to enter with your knees or shoulders exposed, but this rule didn’t seem to be enforced when we were there. It’s best to come in the morning if you want photos from the lower viewpoint near the entrance, as it gets busier later on. The temples look nice from the outside, but the interiors aren’t particularly interesting. There’s also a small photo gallery on site. Afterwards, it’s worth walking to the nearby bridge for a great view of boats on the river with the temple complex in the background. And if you’re hungry, Thap Ba street (running alongside the river near the complex) is basically a “seafood street,” where you can eat tasty seafood dishes for a good price.

reviews.author: Y.H. Kang
reviews.text: One of thw oldest temple in Nha Trang. You can see the locals praying here. Climb 190 steps to see the 23 meter tall budsha.
